places.name: Bowlero Roswell
places.summary: Bowling, arcade games, billiards, food, and lounge seating close to Magnolia.
notes: Very close drive for a short outing.
Website lists wheelchair accessibility.
Best for families who want an activity with seating nearby.

places.name: Olive Garden
places.summary: Casual Italian restaurant with familiar menu items and table service.
notes: Good option for a familiar sit-down meal.
Close enough for a simple lunch or dinner outing.
Call ahead for accessibility, wait times, and seating needs.

places.name: Red Lobster
places.summary: Seafood chain restaurant near Holcomb Bridge Road.
notes: Familiar casual dining choice.
Reasonable distance for family meals.
Confirm current hours and seating before going.

places.name: Pappadeaux Seafood Kitchen
places.summary: Seafood restaurant for a more substantial family meal.
notes: Good for a planned lunch or dinner.
Likely busier than quick casual options.
Call ahead if quieter seating or mobility accommodations are important.

places.name: Rasa Sayang
places.summary: Nearby restaurant option in the Market Boulevard area.
notes: Good candidate for family takeout or a dining outing.
Confirm hours, parking, and seating before visiting.
A little farther than the closest Holcomb Bridge restaurants.

places.name: Buca di Beppo
places.summary: Family-style Italian restaurant on Mansell Road.
notes: Family-style portions work well for group visits.
Best for a planned outing rather than a quick stop.
Call ahead for seating and accessibility questions.

places.name: Jason's Deli
places.summary: Casual deli with soups, sandwiches, salads, and lighter meal options.
notes: Good for a lower-key meal.
Menu usually includes lighter options.
Confirm current location details before visiting.

places.name: Great Clips Centennial Village
places.address: 2300 Holcomb Bridge Rd, Ste 102, Roswell, GA 30076
places.summary: Hair salon with walk-in service and online check-in.
notes: Great Clips advertises senior haircuts.
Online check-in can reduce lobby wait time.
Call or check online before going because wait times can vary.

places.name: North Point Mall
places.summary: Indoor mall with shopping, dining, and an AMC movie theater.
notes: Indoor walking and seating can make visits easier in hot or rainy weather.
Multiple food and shopping options in one stop.
Individual store hours may differ from mall hours.

places.name: Wellstar North Fulton Hospital
notes: Useful reference point for urgent or planned medical care.
Check department location before driving because hospital campuses can be spread out.
Keep emergency needs routed through appropriate medical channels.

places.name: Chattahoochee Nature Center
places.summary: Nature center with outdoor paths, programs, and river-area scenery.
notes: Good for a calm family outing when weather is comfortable.
Check path conditions and accessibility before planning a visit.
Best for residents who enjoy nature and can handle some outdoor walking.

places.name: Mansell Crossing Area
places.address: Mansell Rd near North Point Pkwy, Alpharetta, GA 30022
places.summary: Nearby shopping and restaurant corridor along Mansell Road.
notes: Useful for combining errands with a meal.
Parking and walking distances vary by storefront.
Pick a specific store before leaving to keep the outing simple.

places.name: Big Creek Greenway
places.address: North Point Pkwy at Haynes Bridge Rd, Alpharetta, GA 30022
places.summary: Flat, paved creekside trail with boardwalk stretches, benches, and shade — good for a gentle stroll or wheelchair outing.
notes: Paved and mostly level; benches appear regularly.
Morning visits are cooler and quieter.
Park at the North Point Parkway trailhead for the flattest section.

places.name: Canton Street (Historic Roswell)
places.summary: Historic main street of shops, galleries, porches, and restaurants — a classic slow afternoon with visiting family.
notes: Sidewalks are good but some shops have a step at the door.
Street parking fills on weekends; lots sit one block off Canton.
Plenty of restaurants for a sit-down lunch mid-stroll.

places.name: Bulloch Hall
places.summary: 1839 antebellum home with docent-led tours, gardens, and quiet grounds — an unhurried history outing.
notes: Tours are guided and paced gently; call ahead for accessibility details.
Grounds and gardens are enjoyable even without the house tour.
Pairs well with lunch on nearby Canton Street.

places.name: Roswell Area Park
places.summary: Large city park with a flat walking loop, shade trees, gardens, and plenty of benches near parking.
notes: The main loop near the community center is paved and level.
Restrooms are close to the parking lots.
Good spot for grandkids to burn energy while others sit in the shade.

places.name: Roswell Library
places.summary: Fulton County branch with large-print collections, comfortable seating, and regular daytime programs.
notes: Accessible entrance and parking by the front door.
Ask at the desk about large-print and audiobook sections.
A calm, air-conditioned outing for a hot afternoon.

places.name: Roswell Mill & Vickery Creek Falls
places.summary: Historic mill ruins and a covered bridge overlooking the waterfall — beautiful, but the paths are steep in places.
notes: Trails include stairs and steep grades; best for mobile visitors.
The covered bridge and falls overlook are the shortest walk from parking.
Great photo stop for visiting family.

places.name: Avalon
places.summary: Upscale open-air shopping and dining district with wide flat walkways, fountains, and abundant seating.
notes: Flat, wide sidewalks and plenty of benches throughout.
Valet and garage parking keep walking distances short.
Many restaurants take reservations — helpful for larger family groups.
